meta.name: GHQ-12 General Health Questionnaire
meta.description: The GHQ-12 is a widely used screening instrument for detecting non-psychotic psychiatric disorders and general psychological distress. It consists of 12 items covering feelings of strain, depression, inability to cope, anxiety-based insomnia, and lack of confidence.
meta.author: Goldberg & Williams (1988)
meta.reference: Goldberg DP, Williams P. A User's Guide to the General Health Questionnaire. Windsor: NFER-Nelson; 1988.
meta.instruction: We would like to know how your health has been in general over the past few weeks. Please answer all questions by selecting the answer which you think most nearly applies to you.

questions.text: Been able to concentrate on what you're doing?
Felt you were playing a useful part in things?
Felt capable of making decisions about things?
Felt you couldn't overcome your difficulties?
Been able to enjoy your normal day-to-day activities?
Been able to face up to your problems?
Been thinking of yourself as a worthless person?
Been feeling reasonably happy, all things considered?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, suggesting no significant psychological distress. Your current mental well-being appears stable, and you are likely coping well with everyday demands and stressors.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy habits that support your mental well-being
Keep up regular exercise, social engagement, and adequate sleep
Practice stress management as a preventive measure

ranges.label: Mild to Moderate Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score is above the established caseness threshold of 12, suggesting evidence of psychological distress. You may be experiencing difficulty with concentration, sleep, decision-making, or feeling less confident than usual. These symptoms warrant monitoring and possibly further evaluation.
suggestions: Pay attention to the specific areas where you notice reduced functioning
Ensure you are getting adequate sleep, nutrition, and physical activity
Talk to someone you trust about any concerns or worries
Consider consulting a healthcare professional if symptoms persist beyond a few weeks

ranges.label: Significant Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates significant psychological distress that warrants professional attention. At this level, you are likely experiencing notable difficulties in daily functioning, and a comprehensive evaluation is recommended to identify the underlying issues and appropriate support.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional for a thorough assessment
The GHQ-12 is a general screening tool; further evaluation can identify specific areas of concern
Maintain daily routines and social connections to provide stability
Evidence-based treatments such as counseling or CBT can be very helpful
